devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । तस्यां विदर्भोऽजनयत्पुत्रौ नाम्ना कुशक्रथौ । तृतीयं रोमपादं च विदर्भकुलनन्दनम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācatasyāṃ vidarbho'janayatputrau nāmnā kuśakrathautṛtīyaṃ romapādaṃ ca vidarbhakulanandanam
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Vidarbha begot on her (Bhojyā) two sons by name Kuśa and Kratha, and the third one Ṛomapāda who was the delight of the Vidarbha family.

devanagari: रोमपादसुतो बभ्रुर्बभ्रोः कृतिरजायत । उशिकस्तत्सुतस्तस्माच्चेदिश्चैद्यादयो नृपाः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: romapādasuto babhrurbabhroḥ kṛtirajāyatauśikastatsutastasmāccediścaidyādayo nṛpāḥ
translation: Romapāda’s son was Babhru, from whom was born Kṛti. His son was Uśika from whom proceeded (the line of) kings like Caidya and others (i.e. Damaghoṣa etc.)

devanagari: क्रथस्य कुन्तिः पुत्रोऽभूद्वृष्णिस्तस्याथ निर्वृतिः । ततो दशार्हो नाम्नाभूत्तस्य व्योमः सुतस्ततः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: krathasya kuntiḥ putro'bhūdvṛṣṇistasyātha nirvṛtiḥtato daśārho nāmnābhūttasya vyomaḥ sutastataḥ
translation: Kratha (the second son of Vidarbha) had a son called Kunti, from whom was born Dhṛṣṭi (Vṛṣṇi) whose son was Nirvṛti (Vidhṛti). From him sprang a son named Daśārha, whose son was Vyoma.

devanagari: जीमूतो विकृतिस्तस्य यस्य भीमरथः सुतः । ततो नवरथः पुत्रो जातो दशरथस्ततः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: jīmūto vikṛtistasya yasya bhīmarathaḥ sutaḥtato navarathaḥ putro jāto daśarathastataḥ
translation: His son was Jīmūta whose son was Vikṛti, the father of Bhīmaratha. His son was Navaratha, from whom was born Daśaratha.

devanagari: करम्भिः शकुनेः पुत्रो देवरातस्तदात्मजः । देवक्षत्रस्ततस्तस्य मधुः कुरुवशादनुः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: karambhiḥ śakuneḥ putro devarātastadātmajaḥdevakṣatrastatastasya madhuḥ kuruvaśādanuḥ
translation: From his son Śakuni was born Kerambhi from whose loins appeared Devarāta. His son was Devakṣatra. From him was born Madhu, whose son was Kuruvaśa of whom was born Anu.

devanagari: पुरुहोत्रस्त्वनोः पुत्रस्तस्यायुः सात्वतस्ततः । भजमानो भजिर्दिव्यो वृष्णिर्देवावृधोऽन्धकः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: puruhotrastvanoḥ putrastasyāyuḥ sātvatastataḥbhajamāno bhajirdivyo vṛṣṇirdevāvṛdho'ndhakaḥ
translation: Anu’s son was Puruhotra whose son was Āyu from whom Sātvata was born. Sātvata had seven sons—Bhajamāna, Bhaji, Dīpta, Vṛṣṇi, Devavṛdha, Andhaka, Mahābhoja, Oh noble king! Bhajamāna’s sons were Nimloci, Kiṅkiṇī and Dhṛṣṭi, through one wife, while by another wife, he had three sons viz. Śatājit, Sahasrājit and Ayutājit, Oh Lord.

devanagari: सात्वतस्य सुताः सप्त महाभोजश्च मारिष । भजमानस्य निम्लोचिः किङ्कणो धृष्टिरेव च ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sātvatasya sutāḥ sapta mahābhojaśca māriṣabhajamānasya nimlociḥ kiṅkaṇo dhṛṣṭireva ca
translation: Anu’s son was Puruhotra whose son was Āyu from whom Sātvata was born. Sātvata had seven sons—Bhajamāna, Bhaji, Dīpta, Vṛṣṇi, Devavṛdha, Andhaka, Mahābhoja, Oh noble king! Bhajamāna’s sons were Nimloci, Kiṅkiṇī and Dhṛṣṭi, through one wife, while by another wife, he had three sons viz. Śatājit, Sahasrājit and Ayutājit, Oh Lord.

devanagari: एकस्यामात्मजाः पत्न्यामन्यस्यां च त्रयः सुताः । शताजिच्च सहस्राजिदयुताजिदिति प्रभो ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: ekasyāmātmajāḥ patnyāmanyasyāṃ ca trayaḥ sutāḥśatājicca sahasrājidayutājiditi prabho
translation: Anu’s son was Puruhotra whose son was Āyu from whom Sātvata was born. Sātvata had seven sons—Bhajamāna, Bhaji, Dīpta, Vṛṣṇi, Devavṛdha, Andhaka, Mahābhoja, Oh noble king! Bhajamāna’s sons were Nimloci, Kiṅkiṇī and Dhṛṣṭi, through one wife, while by another wife, he had three sons viz. Śatājit, Sahasrājit and Ayutājit, Oh Lord.

devanagari: बभ्रुर्देवावृधसुतस्तयोः श्लोकौ पठन्त्यमू । यथैव शृणुमो दूरात्सम्पश्यामस्तथान्तिकात् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: babhrurdevāvṛdhasutastayoḥ ślokau paṭhantyamūyathaiva śṛṇumo dūrātsampaśyāmastathāntikāt
translation: Babhru was the son of Devāvṛdha in eulogizing whom the following couplet is recited:

devanagari: बभ्रुः श्रेष्ठो मनुष्याणां देवैर्देवावृधः समः । पुरुषाः पञ्चषष्टिश्च षट्सहस्राणि चाष्ट च ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: babhruḥ śreṣṭho manuṣyāṇāṃ devairdevāvṛdhaḥ samaḥpuruṣāḥ pañcaṣaṣṭiśca ṣaṭsahasrāṇi cāṣṭa ca
translation: Babhru was the son of Devāvṛdha in eulogizing whom the following couplet is recited:

devanagari: येऽमृतत्वमनुप्राप्ता बभ्रोर्देवावृधादपि । महाभोजोऽतिधर्मात्मा भोजा आसंस्तदन्वये ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: ye'mṛtatvamanuprāptā babhrordevāvṛdhādapimahābhojo'tidharmātmā bhojā āsaṃstadanvaye
translation:
Babhru was the son of Devāvṛdha in eulogizing whom the following couplet is recited:
“Just as we hear from far afar, so we see (them) from near. Babhru is the greatest among human beings while Devāvṛdha is just like gods (lit. equal in comparison with gods). Through the spiritual guidance of Babhru and Devāvṛdha fourteen thousand and sixty-five men attained immortality (liberation from saṃsāra.)”
Mahābhoja (the seventh son of Sātvata) was pious, and religious-minded. Kings born in his family-line were (known as) Bhojas.

devanagari: वृष्णेः सुमित्रः पुत्रोऽभूद्युधाजिच्च परन्तप । शिनिस्तस्यानमित्रश्च निघ्नोऽभूदनमित्रतः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛṣṇeḥ sumitraḥ putro'bhūdyudhājicca parantapaśinistasyānamitraśca nighno'bhūdanamitrataḥ
translation: Vṛṣṇi’s son was Sumitra, whose son was Yudhājit, Oh chastiser of enemies. His son was Śini who had Anamitra as his son. From Anamitra appeared Nimna.

devanagari: सत्राजितः प्रसेनश्च निघ्नस्याथासतुः सुतौ । अनमित्रसुतो योऽन्यः शिनिस्तस्य च सत्यकः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: satrājitaḥ prasenaśca nighnasyāthāsatuḥ sutauanamitrasuto yo'nyaḥ śinistasya ca satyakaḥ
translation: Satrājit and Prasena were the two sons of Nimna. Another Śini, a son of Anamitra had a son Satyaka.

devanagari: युयुधानः सात्यकिर्वै जयस्तस्य कुणिस्ततः । युगन्धरोऽनमित्रस्य वृष्णिः पुत्रोऽपरस्ततः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yuyudhānaḥ sātyakirvai jayastasya kuṇistataḥyugandharo'namitrasya vṛṣṇiḥ putro'parastataḥ
translation: Satyaka’s son was Yuyudhāna whose son was Jaya from whom was born Kuṇi. His son was Yugandhara. Anamitra had another son named Vṛṣṇi.

devanagari: श्वफल्कश्चित्ररथश्च गान्दिन्यां च श्वफल्कतः । अक्रूरप्रमुखा आसन्पुत्रा द्वादश विश्रुताः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: śvaphalkaścitrarathaśca gāndinyāṃ ca śvaphalkataḥakrūrapramukhā āsanputrā dvādaśa viśrutāḥ
translation: Vṛṣṇi had two sons—Śvaphalka and Citraratha. Śvaphalka begot on Gandinī twelve famous sons (in addition to Akrūra), but Akrūra was the foremost of them all.

devanagari: आसङ्गः सारमेयश्च मृदुरो मृदुविद्गिरिः । धर्मवृद्धः सुकर्मा च क्षेत्रोपेक्षोऽरिमर्दनः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: āsaṅgaḥ sārameyaśca mṛduro mṛduvidgiriḥdharmavṛddhaḥ sukarmā ca kṣetropekṣo'rimardanaḥ
translation: (The names of those sons are:) Āsaṅga, Sārameya, Mṛdura, Mṛduvid, Giri, Dharmavṛddha, Sukarmā, Kṣetropekṣa, Arimardana, Śatrughna, Gandhamāda and Pratibāhu. They had a sister called Sucīrā. Akrūra also had two sons—

devanagari: शत्रुघ्नो गन्धमादश्च प्रतिबाहुश्च द्वादश । तेषां स्वसा सुचाराख्या द्वावक्रूरसुतावपि ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śatrughno gandhamādaśca pratibāhuśca dvādaśateṣāṃ svasā sucārākhyā dvāvakrūrasutāvapi
translation: (The names of those sons are:) Āsaṅga, Sārameya, Mṛdura, Mṛduvid, Giri, Dharmavṛddha, Sukarmā, Kṣetropekṣa, Arimardana, Śatrughna, Gandhamāda and Pratibāhu. They had a sister called Sucīrā. Akrūra also had two sons—

devanagari: देववानुपदेवश्च तथा चित्ररथात्मजाः । पृथुर्विदूरथाद्याश्च बहवो वृष्णिनन्दनाः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: devavānupadevaśca tathā citrarathātmajāḥpṛthurvidūrathādyāśca bahavo vṛṣṇinandanāḥ
translation: Viz. Devavān and Upadeva. Citraratha had sons such as Pṛthu, Vidūra and many others—all descendants of Vṛṣṇi.

devanagari: कुकुरो भजमानश्च शुचिः कम्बलबर्हिषः । कुकुरस्य सुतो वह्निर्विलोमा तनयस्ततः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kukuro bhajamānaśca śuciḥ kambalabarhiṣaḥkukurasya suto vahnirvilomā tanayastataḥ
translation: Andhaka[1] had four sons: Kukura, Bhajamāna, Śuci and Kambalabarhiṣa. Kukura’s son was Vahni, whose son was Viloma.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
On the strength of Viṣṇu Purāṇa 4.14.12 Bhāvāratha Dīpikā gives the name Andhaka, though Bhāgavata Purāṇa does not mention his name.

devanagari: कपोतरोमा तस्यानुः सखा यस्य च तुम्बुरुः । अन्धकाद्दुन्दुभिस्तस्मादविद्योतः पुनर्वसुः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: kapotaromā tasyānuḥ sakhā yasya ca tumburuḥandhakāddundubhistasmādavidyotaḥ punarvasuḥ
translation: He had a son Kapotaroma whose son was Anu. His friend was Tumbaru (the famous celestial musician. From Anu was born Andhaka, from whom was born Dundubhi. His son was Aridyota, the father of Punarvasu.

devanagari: तस्याहुकश्चाहुकी च कन्या चैवाहुकात्मजौ । देवकश्चोग्रसेनश्च चत्वारो देवकात्मजाः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāhukaścāhukī ca kanyā caivāhukātmajaudevakaścograsenaśca catvāro devakātmajāḥ
translation: Punarvasu had a son called Āhuka, and a daughter named Āhukī. Āhuka’s sons were Devaka and Ugrasena. Devaka had four sons:

devanagari: देववानुपदेवश्च सुदेवो देववर्धनः । तेषां स्वसारः सप्तासन्धृतदेवादयो नृप ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: devavānupadevaśca sudevo devavardhanaḥteṣāṃ svasāraḥ saptāsandhṛtadevādayo nṛpa
translation: Viz. Devavān, Upadeva, Sudeva, Devavardhana. They had seven sisters such as Dhṛtadevā and others, Oh King!

devanagari: शान्तिदेवोपदेवा च श्रीदेवा देवरक्षिता । सहदेवा देवकी च वसुदेव उवाह ताः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śāntidevopadevā ca śrīdevā devarakṣitāsahadevā devakī ca vasudeva uvāha tāḥ
translation: (In addition to Dhṛtadevā) the sisters were Śāntidevā, Upadevā, Śrīdevā, Devarakṣitā, Sahadevā and Devakī all of whom Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa’s father) married.

devanagari: कंसः सुनामा न्यग्रोधः कङ्कः शङ्कुः सुहूस्तथा । राष्ट्रपालोऽथ धृष्टिश्च तुष्टिमानौग्रसेनयः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃsaḥ sunāmā nyagrodhaḥ kaṅkaḥ śaṅkuḥ suhūstathārāṣṭrapālo'tha dhṛṣṭiśca tuṣṭimānaugrasenayaḥ
translation: Kaṃsa, Sunāma, Nyagrodha, Kaṅka, Śaṅku, Suhū, Rāṣṭrapāla, Śṛṣṭi [Sṛṣṭi?] and Tuṣṭimān were the sons of Ugrasena.

devanagari: कंसा कंसवती कङ्का शूरभू राष्टपालिका । उग्रसेनदुहितरो वसुदेवानुजस्त्रियः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃsā kaṃsavatī kaṅkā śūrabhū rāṣṭapālikāugrasenaduhitaro vasudevānujastriyaḥ
translation: The daughters of Ugrasena were Kaṃsā, Kaṃsāvatī, Kaṅkā, Śarabhū, Rāṣṭrapālikā, and they were the wives of Vasudeva’s younger brothers.

devanagari: शूरो विदूरथादासीद्भजमानस्तु तत्सुतः । शिनिस्तस्मात्स्वयं भोजो हृदिकस्तत्सुतो मतः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śūro vidūrathādāsīdbhajamānastu tatsutaḥśinistasmātsvayaṃ bhojo hṛdikastatsuto mataḥ
translation: Śūra was born of Vidūratha (the son of Citraratha). His son was Bhajamāna of whom was born Śini. From him was born Svayambhoja whose son was Hṛdika.

devanagari: देवमीढः शतधनुः कृतवर्मेति तत्सुताः । देवमीढस्य शूरस्य मारिषा नाम पत्न्यभूत् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: devamīḍhaḥ śatadhanuḥ kṛtavarmeti tatsutāḥdevamīḍhasya śūrasya māriṣā nāma patnyabhūt
translation: Hṛdika’s sons were Devabāhu, Śatadhanu, and Kṛtavarmā. Śūra (Śūrasena) was the son of Devamīḍha (who, as explained by Bhāvāratha Dīpikā on the strength of Parāśara i.e. Viṣṇu Purāṇa to be the fourth son of Hṛdika[2]). Māriṣā was Śūra’s wife.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
The name however does not appear in the text of the Viṣṇu Purāṇa 4.14.24 in the Gita Press edition. Saṃvat 2026. But Bhāvāratha Dīpikā clearly states:
devamīḍhasya yaḥ śūro nāma putras tasya devamīḍho hṛdikasyaiva sutas tathā'ha parāśaraḥ /
And quotes Viṣṇu Purāṇa 4.14.24 etc.

devanagari: तस्यां स जनयामास दश पुत्रानकल्मषान् । वसुदेवं देवभागं देवश्रवसमानकम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ sa janayāmāsa daśa putrānakalmaṣānvasudevaṃ devabhāgaṃ devaśravasamānakam
translation: He begot on her ten sons of spotless character viz. Vasudeva, Devabhāga, Devaśravas, Anaka, Sṛñjaya, Śyāmaka, Kaṅka, Śamīka, Vatsaka, Vṛka. They call Vasudeva, the father of Hari (lit. the source of Hari’s birth), as Anakadundubhi, as the celestial kettledrums (dundubhis) and still bigger drums (Ānakas) were sounded at his (auspicious) birth. And their five sisters Pṛthā, Śrutadevā, Śrutakīrti, Śrutaśravas.

devanagari: सृञ्जयं श्यामकं कङ्कं शमीकं वत्सकं वृकम् । देवदुन्दुभयो नेदुरानका यस्य जन्मनि ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sṛñjayaṃ śyāmakaṃ kaṅkaṃ śamīkaṃ vatsakaṃ vṛkamdevadundubhayo nedurānakā yasya janmani
translation: He begot on her ten sons of spotless character viz. Vasudeva, Devabhāga, Devaśravas, Anaka, Sṛñjaya, Śyāmaka, Kaṅka, Śamīka, Vatsaka, Vṛka. They call Vasudeva, the father of Hari (lit. the source of Hari’s birth), as Anakadundubhi, as the celestial kettledrums (dundubhis) and still bigger drums (Ānakas) were sounded at his (auspicious) birth. And their five sisters Pṛthā, Śrutadevā, Śrutakīrti, Śrutaśravas.

devanagari: वसुदेवं हरेः स्थानं वदन्त्यानकदुन्दुभिम् । पृथा च श्रुतदेवा च श्रुतकीर्तिः श्रुतश्रवाः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: vasudevaṃ hareḥ sthānaṃ vadantyānakadundubhimpṛthā ca śrutadevā ca śrutakīrtiḥ śrutaśravāḥ
translation: He begot on her ten sons of spotless character viz. Vasudeva, Devabhāga, Devaśravas, Anaka, Sṛñjaya, Śyāmaka, Kaṅka, Śamīka, Vatsaka, Vṛka. They call Vasudeva, the father of Hari (lit. the source of Hari’s birth), as Anakadundubhi, as the celestial kettledrums (dundubhis) and still bigger drums (Ānakas) were sounded at his (auspicious) birth. And their five sisters Pṛthā, Śrutadevā, Śrutakīrti, Śrutaśravas.

devanagari: राजाधिदेवी चैतेषां भगिन्यः पञ्च कन्यकाः । कुन्तेः सख्युः पिता शूरो ह्यपुत्रस्य पृथामदात् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: rājādhidevī caiteṣāṃ bhaginyaḥ pañca kanyakāḥkunteḥ sakhyuḥ pitā śūro hyaputrasya pṛthāmadāt
translation: And Rājādhidevī were his five daughters. (Pṛthā’s) father Śūra gave her in adoption to his friend Kunti who was childless,

devanagari: साप दुर्वाससो विद्यां देवहूतीं प्रतोषितात् । तस्या वीर्यपरीक्षार्थमाजुहाव रविं शुचिः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: sāpa durvāsaso vidyāṃ devahūtīṃ pratoṣitāttasyā vīryaparīkṣārthamājuhāva raviṃ śuciḥ
translation: From the sage Durvāsas whom she pleased (by her services), she obtained the lore (mantras) of invoking gods (Devahutī Vidyā). For testing the efficacy of that lore, she invoked the lustrous sun.

devanagari: तदैवोपागतं देवं वीक्ष्य विस्मितमानसा । प्रत्ययार्थं प्रयुक्ता मे याहि देव क्षमस्व मे ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tadaivopāgataṃ devaṃ vīkṣya vismitamānasāpratyayārthaṃ prayuktā me yāhi deva kṣamasva me
translation: She was astounded to see the (sun)-god appearing instantly in her presence. (Being embarrassed, apologetically) she said, “It is just for testing the efficacy of the lore that the mantra was used. Excuse me, Oh god, and (kindly) go your way.”

devanagari: अमोघं देवसन्दर्शमादधे त्वयि चात्मजम् । योनिर्यथा न दुष्येत कर्ताहं ते सुमध्यमे ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: amoghaṃ devasandarśamādadhe tvayi cātmajamyoniryathā na duṣyeta kartāhaṃ te sumadhyame
translation: ‘The sight of gods is never fruitless, Oh lady! I shall beget a son for you, and see to it that your virginity is unimpaired, Oh girl of beautiful waist!’

devanagari: इति तस्यां स आधाय गर्भं सूर्यो दिवं गतः । सद्यः कुमारः सञ्जज्ञे द्वितीय इव भास्करः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti tasyāṃ sa ādhāya garbhaṃ sūryo divaṃ gataḥsadyaḥ kumāraḥ sañjajñe dvitīya iva bhāskaraḥ
translation: Assuring her thus, the sun-god impregnated her and returned to the firmament. She instantly gave birth to a son who was (brilliant) like another sun.

devanagari: तं सात्यजन्नदीतोये कृच्छ्राल्लोकस्य बिभ्यती । प्रपितामहस्तामुवाह पाण्डुर्वै सत्यविक्रमः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ sātyajannadītoye kṛcchrāllokasya bibhyatīprapitāmahastāmuvāha pāṇḍurvai satyavikramaḥ
translation: Being afraid of a public censure, she abandoned him (in a box) in the river. Your really valorous great grandfather Pāṇḍu married her.

devanagari: श्रुतदेवां तु कारूषो वृद्धशर्मा समग्रहीत् । यस्यामभूद्दन्तवक्र ऋषिशप्तो दितेः सुतः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutadevāṃ tu kārūṣo vṛddhaśarmā samagrahītyasyāmabhūddantavakra ṛṣiśapto diteḥ sutaḥ
translation: Vṛddhaśarmā of Kāruṣa dynasty married Śrutadevā of whom was born Dantavakra, one of the Diti’s sons (viz. Hiraṇyākṣa) who was formerly attendant of god Viṣṇu, called Vijaya but cursed by the sage (Sanaka).

devanagari: कैकेयो धृष्टकेतुश्च श्रुतकीर्तिमविन्दत । सन्तर्दनादयस्तस्यां पञ्चासन्कैकयाः सुताः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: kaikeyo dhṛṣṭaketuśca śrutakīrtimavindatasantardanādayastasyāṃ pañcāsankaikayāḥ sutāḥ
translation: Dhṛṣṭaketu of Kekaya family wedded Śrutakīrti and had five sons in the Kekaya family.

devanagari: राजाधिदेव्यामावन्त्यौ जयसेनोऽजनिष्ट ह । दमघोषश्चेदिराजः श्रुतश्रवसमग्रहीत् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: rājādhidevyāmāvantyau jayaseno'janiṣṭa hadamaghoṣaścedirājaḥ śrutaśravasamagrahīt
translation: Jayasena begot by Rājādhidevī two sons viz. Vinda and Anuvinda. Damaghoṣa, the king of Cedi, married Śrutaśravā.

devanagari: शिशुपालः सुतस्तस्याः कथितस्तस्य सम्भवः । देवभागस्य कंसायां चित्रकेतुबृहद्बलौ ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: śiśupālaḥ sutastasyāḥ kathitastasya sambhavaḥdevabhāgasya kaṃsāyāṃ citraketubṛhadbalau
translation: Śiśupāla was her (Śrutaśravā’s) son. Thus the (account of his) birth is narrated. By Kaṃsā, Devabhāga had two sons, Citraketu and Bṛhadbala.

devanagari: कंसवत्यां देवश्रवसः सुवीर इषुमांस्तथा । बकः कङ्कात्तु कङ्कायां सत्यजित्पुरुजित्तथा ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃsavatyāṃ devaśravasaḥ suvīra iṣumāṃstathābakaḥ kaṅkāttu kaṅkāyāṃ satyajitpurujittathā
translation: Devaśravas had by Kaṃsavatī two sons—Suvīra and Iṣumān, while Ānaka begot on Kaṅkā (two sons viz.) Satyajit and Purujit.

devanagari: सृञ्जयो राष्ट्रपाल्यां च वृषदुर्मर्षणादिकान् । हरिकेशहिरण्याक्षौ शूरभूम्यां च श्यामकः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sṛñjayo rāṣṭrapālyāṃ ca vṛṣadurmarṣaṇādikānharikeśahiraṇyākṣau śūrabhūmyāṃ ca śyāmakaḥ
translation: Sṛñjaya procreated from Rāṣṭrapālī, Vṛṣa, Durmarṣaṇa and other sons, while Śyāmaka had Harikeśa and Hiraṇyākṣa by Śūrabhūmī.

devanagari: मिश्रकेश्यामप्सरसि वृकादीन्वत्सकस्तथा । तक्षपुष्करशालादीन्दुर्वाक्ष्यां वृक आदधे ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: miśrakeśyāmapsarasi vṛkādīnvatsakastathātakṣapuṣkaraśālādīndurvākṣyāṃ vṛka ādadhe
translation: Vatsaka begot on a celestial nymph Miśrakeśī, Vṛka and other sons, while Vṛka had by his wife Durvākṣī sons such as Takṣa. Puṣkara, Śāla and others.

devanagari: सुमित्रार्जुनपालादीन्समीकात्तु सुदामनी । आनकः कर्णिकायां वै ऋतधामाजयावपि ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: sumitrārjunapālādīnsamīkāttu sudāmanīānakaḥ karṇikāyāṃ vai ṛtadhāmājayāvapi
translation: By Sudāminī, Śamīka had Sumitra, Arjunapāla and other sons, while Kaṅka begot on Karṇikā, Ṛtadhāman and Jaya.

devanagari: पौरवी रोहिणी भद्रा मदिरा रोचना इला । देवकीप्रमुखाश्चासन्पत्न्य आनकदुन्दुभेः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: pauravī rohiṇī bhadrā madirā rocanā ilādevakīpramukhāścāsanpatnya ānakadundubheḥ
translation: Ānakadundubhi (Vasudeva) had Pauravī, Rohiṇī, Bhadrā, Madirā, Rocanā, Ilā and Devakī (and others) as wives. Out of them Devakī was prominent.

devanagari: बलं गदं सारणं च दुर्मदं विपुलं ध्रुवम् । वसुदेवस्तु रोहिण्यां कृतादीनुदपादयत् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: balaṃ gadaṃ sāraṇaṃ ca durmadaṃ vipulaṃ dhruvamvasudevastu rohiṇyāṃ kṛtādīnudapādayat
translation: By Rohiṇī, Vasudeva procreated Bala, Gada, Sāraṇa, Durmada, Vipula, Dhruva, as well as Kṛta and others.

devanagari: सुभद्रो भद्र बाहुश्च दुर्मदो भद्र एव च । पौरव्यास्तनया ह्येते भूताद्या द्वादशाभवन् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: subhadro bhadra bāhuśca durmado bhadra eva capauravyāstanayā hyete bhūtādyā dvādaśābhavan
translation: Vasudeva’s sons by Pauravī were Subhadra, Bhadravāha, Durmada, Bhadra as well as others beginning with. Bhūta, all together being twelve (in number).

devanagari: नन्दोपनन्दकृतक शूराद्या मदिरात्मजाः । कौशल्या केशिनं त्वेकमसूत कुलनन्दनम् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: nandopanandakṛtaka śūrādyā madirātmajāḥkauśalyā keśinaṃ tvekamasūta kulanandanam
translation: Nanda, Upananda, Kṛtaka, Śūra and others were the children of (Vasudeva’s wife) Madirā, while Kausalyā gave birth to one son Keśin, the delight of his family.

devanagari: रोचनायामतो जाता हस्तहेमाङ्गदादयः । इलायामुरुवल्कादीन्यदुमुख्यानजीजनत् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: rocanāyāmato jātā hastahemāṅgadādayaḥilāyāmuruvalkādīnyadumukhyānajījanat
translation: Rocanā bore (to Vasudeva) Hasta, Hemāṅgada and other sons. He begot on Ilā, Uruvalka and other chiefs of Yadus.

devanagari: विपृष्ठो धृतदेवायामेक आनकदुन्दुभेः । शान्तिदेवात्मजा राजन्प्रशमप्रसितादयः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: vipṛṣṭho dhṛtadevāyāmeka ānakadundubheḥśāntidevātmajā rājanpraśamaprasitādayaḥ
translation: Ānakadundubhi (Vasudeva) had only one son Vipṛṣṭha from Dhṛtadevā, while the sons of Sāntidevā [Śāntidevā?] were Śrama, Pratiśruta and others, Oh king!

devanagari: राजन्यकल्पवर्षाद्या उपदेवासुता दश । वसुहंससुवंशाद्याः श्रीदेवायास्तु षट्सुताः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: rājanyakalpavarṣādyā upadevāsutā daśavasuhaṃsasuvaṃśādyāḥ śrīdevāyāstu ṣaṭsutāḥ
translation: Ten kings beginning with Kalpavarṣa were the sons of Upadevā; while Śrīdevā had six sons viz. Vasu, Haṃsa, Suvaṃśa and (three) others.

devanagari: देवरक्षितया लब्धा नव चात्र गदादयः । वसुदेवः सुतानष्टावादधे सहदेवया ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: devarakṣitayā labdhā nava cātra gadādayaḥvasudevaḥ sutānaṣṭāvādadhe sahadevayā
translation: By Devarakṣitā, he got nine sons such as Gada and others. While he begot eight sons on Sahadevā. Of them, Puruviśruta was most prominent. Just as God Dharma procreated eight Vasus, the noble-minded Vasudeva begot on Devakī eight sons.

devanagari: प्रवरश्रतमुख्यांश्च साक्षाद्धर्मो वसूनिव । वसुदेवस्तु देवक्यामष्ट पुत्रानजीजनत् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: pravaraśratamukhyāṃśca sākṣāddharmo vasūnivavasudevastu devakyāmaṣṭa putrānajījanat
translation: By Devarakṣitā, he got nine sons such as Gada and others. While he begot eight sons on Sahadevā. Of them, Puruviśruta was most prominent. Just as God Dharma procreated eight Vasus, the noble-minded Vasudeva begot on Devakī eight sons.

devanagari: कीर्तिमन्तं सुषेणं च भद्रसेनमुदारधीः । ऋजुं सम्मर्दनं भद्रं सङ्कर्षणमहीश्वरम् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: kīrtimantaṃ suṣeṇaṃ ca bhadrasenamudāradhīḥṛjuṃ sammardanaṃ bhadraṃ saṅkarṣaṇamahīśvaram
translation: They were Kīrtimat, Suṣeṇa, Bhadrasena, Ṛju, Sammardana, Bhadra and Saṅkarṣaṇa who was Śeṣa, the Lord of Serpents.

devanagari: अष्टमस्तु तयोरासीत्स्वयमेव हरिः किल । सुभद्रा च महाभागा तव राजन्पितामही ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: aṣṭamastu tayorāsītsvayameva hariḥ kilasubhadrā ca mahābhāgā tava rājanpitāmahī
translation: Their eighth son was verily Lord Hari himself. And the highly fortunate Subhadrā, your grand mother was their daughter, Oh King!

devanagari: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य क्षयो वृद्धिश्च पाप्मनः । तदा तु भगवानीश आत्मानं सृजते हरिः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā yadā hi dharmasya kṣayo vṛddhiśca pāpmanaḥtadā tu bhagavānīśa ātmānaṃ sṛjate hariḥ
translation: Whenever there is deterioration in righteousness and waxing of sinfulness, the glorious Lord Hari incarnates himself.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Cf. yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata /
abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṃ sṛjāmyaham // Bh. GĪTĀ

devanagari: न ह्यस्य जन्मनो हेतुः कर्मणो वा महीपते । आत्ममायां विनेशस्य परस्य द्र ष्टुरात्मनः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyasya janmano hetuḥ karmaṇo vā mahīpateātmamāyāṃ vineśasya parasya dra ṣṭurātmanaḥ
translation: Oh King! No cause except his will-power (Māyā) can be attributed for the appearance or deeds of the Lord who is the all-pervading Lord of the universe, the Supreme- most, detached and impartial Witness.

devanagari: यन्मायाचेष्टितं पुंसः स्थित्युत्पत्त्यप्ययाय हि । अनुग्रहस्तन्निवृत्तेरात्मलाभाय चेष्यते ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: yanmāyāceṣṭitaṃ puṃsaḥ sthityutpattyapyayāya hianugrahastannivṛtterātmalābhāya ceṣyate
translation: The activity of the Lord through his Māyā brings about the birth, continuation and destruction of the jīva (an embodied soul). The recession of Māyā is his Grace, which leads to self-realization.

devanagari: अक्षौहिणीनां पतिभिरसुरैर्नृपलाञ्छनैः । भुव आक्रम्यमाणाया अभाराय कृतोद्यमः ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: akṣauhiṇīnāṃ patibhirasurairnṛpalāñchanaiḥbhuva ākramyamāṇāyā abhārāya kṛtodyamaḥ
translation: He endeavoured to lighten the burden of the earth which was being over-run by Asuras in the guise of kings leading Akṣauhiṇīs of army.

devanagari: कर्माण्यपरिमेयाणि मनसापि सुरेश्वरैः । सहसङ्कर्षणश्चक्रे भगवान्मधुसूदनः ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: karmāṇyaparimeyāṇi manasāpi sureśvaraiḥsahasaṅkarṣaṇaścakre bhagavānmadhusūdanaḥ
translation: The glorious Lord Viṣṇu (Slayer of demon Madhu) accompanied by Saṅkarṣaṇa (Balarāma) achieved feats which the Lords of gods could not comprehend, even by thought or imagination.

devanagari: कलौ जनिष्यमाणानां दुःखशोकतमोनुदम् । अनुग्रहाय भक्तानां सुपुण्यं व्यतनोद्यशः ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: kalau janiṣyamāṇānāṃ duḥkhaśokatamonudamanugrahāya bhaktānāṃ supuṇyaṃ vyatanodyaśaḥ
translation: For conferring grace on his devotees, he spread his hallowing glory which will also remove the misery, sorrow and ignorance of those who will be born in (the impending) Kali age.

devanagari: यस्मिन्सत्कर्णपीयुषे यशस्तीर्थवरे सकृत् । श्रोत्राञ्जलिरुपस्पृश्य धुनुते कर्मवासनाम् ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: yasminsatkarṇapīyuṣe yaśastīrthavare sakṛtśrotrāñjalirupaspṛśya dhunute karmavāsanām
translation: Having but once slightly tasted with the joined palms in the form of ears, from the sacred-most stream of his glory, which is sweet like nectar to the ears of the righteous people, a person shakes himself free from tendencies to Karmas.

devanagari: भोजवृष्ण्यन्धकमधु शूरसेनदशार्हकैः । श्लाघनीयेहितः शश्वत्कुरुसृञ्जयपाण्डुभिः ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhojavṛṣṇyandhakamadhu śūrasenadaśārhakaiḥślāghanīyehitaḥ śaśvatkurusṛñjayapāṇḍubhiḥ
translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa whose sportive acts were ever eulogised by (the members of the clans like) Bhojas, Vṛṣṇis, Andhakas, Madhus, Śūrasenas and Daśārhas as well as Kauravas, Sṛñjayas and Pāṇḍavas, delighted the entire humanity with his affectionate smiles and glances, with his sublime speech and his valorous sportive actions and personality, charming in every respect.

devanagari: स्निग्धस्मितेक्षितोदारैर्वाक्यैर्विक्रमलीलया । नृलोकं रमयामास मूर्त्या सर्वाङ्गरम्यया ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: snigdhasmitekṣitodārairvākyairvikramalīlayānṛlokaṃ ramayāmāsa mūrtyā sarvāṅgaramyayā
translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa whose sportive acts were ever eulogised by (the members of the clans like) Bhojas, Vṛṣṇis, Andhakas, Madhus, Śūrasenas and Daśārhas as well as Kauravas, Sṛñjayas and Pāṇḍavas, delighted the entire humanity with his affectionate smiles and glances, with his sublime speech and his valorous sportive actions and personality, charming in every respect.

devanagari: यस्याननं मकरकुण्डलचारुकर्ण भ्राजत्कपोलसुभगं सविलासहासम् । नित्योत्सवं न ततृपुर्दृशिभिः पिबन्त्यो नार्यो नराश्च मुदिताः कुपिता निमेश्च ॥...
english_devnagari: yasyānanaṃ makarakuṇḍalacārukarṇa bhrājatkapolasubhagaṃ savilāsahāsamnityotsavaṃ na tatṛpurdṛśibhiḥ pibantyo nāryo narāśca muditāḥ kupitā nimeśca
translation: Drinking (as it were) with their eyes, his countenance appearing most beautiful with ears ornamented with alligator-shaped ear-rings and with brilliant cheeks, a face beaming with graceful sportive smiles, an eternal seat of ecstasy—men and women[4] steeped in delight, are never satiated but grow angry with Nimi (who causes winking and thus deprives them of their happiness and joy).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
VC. insists that these were cowherd women of Vṛṇdāvana and not others, and men were Kṛṣṇa’s mates like Subala and quotes Ujjvala- nīlamaṇi as an authority on this.

devanagari: जातो गतः पितृगृहाद्व्रजमेधितार्थो हत्वा रिपून्सुतशतानि कृतोरुदारः । उत्पाद्य तेषु पुरुषः क्रतुभिः समीजे आत्मानमात्मनिगमं प्रथयन्जनेषु ॥...
english_devnagari: jāto gataḥ pitṛgṛhādvrajamedhitārtho hatvā ripūnsutaśatāni kṛtorudāraḥutpādya teṣu puruṣaḥ kratubhiḥ samīje ātmānamātmanigamaṃ prathayanjaneṣu
translation: He manifested himself with his own form, but later on assuming the human form, he went from his father’s house to Vraja. He enhanced its prosperity. He killed all the enemies; he married a number of wives and begot hundreds of sons through them. He worshipped the Supreme person (who is himself) by performing numerous sacrifices, and promulgated thereby the Vedic path that leads to him, amongst the people.

devanagari: पृथ्व्याः स वै गुरुभरं क्षपयन्कुरूणामन्तःसमुत्थकलिना युधि भूपचम्वः । दृष्ट्या विधूय विजये जयमुद्विघोष्य प्रोच्योद्धवाय च परं समगात्स्वध...
english_devnagari: pṛthvyāḥ sa vai gurubharaṃ kṣapayankurūṇāmantaḥsamutthakalinā yudhi bhūpacamvaḥdṛṣṭyā vidhūya vijaye jayamudvighoṣya procyoddhavāya ca paraṃ samagātsvadhāma
translation:
Desiring to reduce the heavy burden of the earth, he struck down with his glance the legions of kings in the battle resulting from the internal feuds of the Kuru clans, but (gave credit of victory to Arjuna) by proclaiming the victory (as if) won by Arjuna; he then enlightened Uddhava in the secrets of the highest Truth, and retired to his Supreme Abode.
